title: Baklava
subtitle: Layers of filo pastry with spiced walnuts, soaked in honey syrup
excerpt: Baklava: layers of filo pastry brushed with melted butter, filled with spiced ground walnuts, baked until crisp and golden, then drenched in a honey and lemon syrup while the pastry is still hot. The contrast between the cold syrup and hot baklava is what makes it work.
sourceNotes: Baklava is a layered pastry from the Ottoman confectionery tradition documented from at least the fifteenth century in Turkish culinary records. It appears across Turkish, Greek, Lebanese, and broader Middle Eastern cookery in many regional variations. Charles Perry's historical analysis of Ottoman palace recipes and Claudia Roden, A Book of Middle Eastern Food (1968), document the canonical form and its regional variations. The walnut, honey, and filo version described here is the most widely reproduced domestic form and is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Improves over 24 hours as the syrup is fully absorbed. Keeps at room temperature for up to 5 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 40 to 50 minutes until deep golden

glossaryTerms.definition: When pouring syrup over baklava, the pastry should be hot and the syrup cold (or at room temperature). This temperature contrast is important: hot syrup on hot pastry causes the layers to steam and go soggy. Cold syrup poured over hot pastry is absorbed slowly and evenly, and the layers stay distinct. Make the syrup first and let it cool to room temperature before baking. Pour it over the baklava as soon as the tray comes out of the oven.

content.text: Baklava is labour-intensive in its layering but otherwise simple. The filo dries fast — keep unused sheets under a damp cloth. Work quickly and use plenty of butter between each layer.

Combine the {{honey}}, {{caster-sugar}} (syrup portion), 150 ml water, and the juice of the {{lemon}} in a saucepan. Stir over medium heat until the sugar dissolves, then simmer for 10 minutes. Set aside to cool completely. See the
rule: this must be cold before you pour it.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Mix the {{walnuts}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, and {{caster-sugar}} (filling portion) together. Brush a 30 × 20 cm baking tin with melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Layer 6 sheets of {{filo-pastry}}, brushing each with butter. Spread half the walnut mixture evenly over the top. Layer 3 more filo sheets, each buttered. Spread the remaining walnut mixture. Finish with the remaining filo sheets, buttering each layer including the top. Cut through all layers into diamonds or squares before baking.

Bake for 40 to 50 minutes until deep golden brown. Remove from the oven and immediately pour the cold syrup evenly over the hot baklava. Leave to cool completely at room temperature for at least 2 hours before serving, retracing the cuts as needed.
